topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam stared at the application portal, his jaw tight enough to crack. He’d spent three weeks polishing his resume, tailoring his cover letter to the precise wording of the job description. Then, he saw it: Sarah’s name, submitted just an hour before his own. A slow, hot flush crept up his neck. He slammed his laptop shut, the sound echoing sharply in the quiet apartment. "Seriously?" he muttered to the empty room, running a hand through his already messy hair. He felt a strange, prickly heat building in his chest, a refusal to accept the obvious. He pulled up Sarah’s profile, the success metrics glowing brightly. It was unfair. Utterly, monumentally unfair.

[story 2]

"You actually submitted yours?" Maya asked, her voice flat, as Liam watched the confirmation email pop up on his screen. He felt a sudden, sharp tightening in his shoulders, a physical recoil. He leaned back in his chair, crossing his arms tightly over his chest, trying to keep his expression neutral. "Of course, I did," he managed, the words sounding brittle even to his own ears. He felt a distinct, unpleasant clenching low in his stomach. He glanced over at Sarah, who was already celebrating, a wide, unrestrained smile spreading across her face. The sheer audacity of it made his breath catch, a shallow, quick intake of air.

[story 3]

I reread the job posting for the tenth time, my eyes scanning the fine print, searching for some overlooked clause, some tiny discrepancy that would justify my own candidacy. When I finally clicked 'submit,' a wave of profound dissatisfaction washed over me. It wasn't anger, exactly; it was a heavy, suffocating sense of being completely overlooked, of having poured everything into this opportunity only to have it snatched away by someone else. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small living room floor. My footsteps were quick, uneven. I felt my knuckles whiten as I gripped the edge of the desk, the tension vibrating through my entire frame. Why couldn't they see *my* qualifications?
